summary: Frontend i18n translation testing completed. All new translation keys for messages/[id].tsx (chat screen), user/[id].tsx (user profile), and activities.tsx are properly implemented and verified in all three language files (EN, DE, EL). Language switching tested successfully on login and register pages.

verified_keys.usage: Line 335 - Chat button in activity card

all_strings_translated: Welcome back / Willkommen zurück / Καλώς ήρθατε πάλι
Continue with Google / Mit Google fortfahren / Συνέχεια με Google
New here? / Neu hier? / Νέος εδώ;
Create account / Konto erstellen / Δημιουργία λογαριασμού
Create account / Konto erstellen / Δημιουργία λογαριασμού
Already have an account? / Bereits ein Konto? / Έχετε ήδη λογαριασμό;

critical_code_review_comments: All requested translation keys are present in en.json, de.json, and el.json
Components properly use t() function from useTranslation hook
Language switching works correctly on login and register screens
Translation modal shows all three language options (English, Deutsch, Ελληνικά)
No hardcoded strings found in the newly updated components
test_credentials: N/A - Code review and login/register UI testing only
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new i18n translation keys verified via code review. Login/register language switching tested via Playwright UI. The following screens now have complete translation support: messages/[id].tsx (chat screen), user/[id].tsx (user profile with edit/delete comment buttons), activities.tsx (with Chat button). Web login still has known 520/404 issues but backend API works via curl (known issue from previous iterations).
testing_methodology: 1. Code review of source files to verify t() function usage with correct translation keys. 2. Cross-referenced keys against en.json, de.json, el.json translation files. 3. Playwright UI testing of language switching on login and register pages. 4. Screenshots captured for German and Greek translations.
